docs.text: Welcome to Material for MkDocs.

docs.title: ewwii_log
docs.text:
void ewwii_log(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * msg)

Log a message.

Log a message to ewwii with the appropriate plugin ID visible.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • msg The message to log

docs.title: ewwii_inject_css
docs.text:
void ewwii_inject_css(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * css, void(*)(const struct HostHandle *, uint64_t *) future_handler)

Inject custom CSS.

Inject CSS into the core ewwii engine and handle the resulting CSS ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • css The css string to inject @future_handler A function to call when the CSS ID is resolved

docs.title: ewwii_remove_css
docs.text:
void ewwii_remove_css(const struct HostHandle * handle, uint64_t * idx_ptr)

Remove an injected CSS.

Remove an injected CSS from ewwii using the resolved CSS ID.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • idx_ptr The pointer to the resolved CSS ID which is to be removed

docs.title: ewwii_inject_nbcl
docs.text:
void ewwii_inject_nbcl(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * nbcl)

Inject nbcl into ewwii.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • nbcl The nbcl code to inject

docs.title: ewwii_get_runtime_paths
docs.text:
void ewwii_get_runtime_paths(const struct HostHandle * handle, void(*)(const struct HostHandle *, const struct CRuntimePaths *) future_handler)

Get the runtime paths.

Get the runtime paths like the configuration directory, socket file, etc.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: • handle The host handle @future_handler The function to call when the CRuntimePaths are resolved

docs.title: ewwii_emit
docs.text:
void ewwii_emit(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * signal, const char * data)

Emit a message.

Emit a message which other plugins can see and work with the provided data.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: • handle The host handle @signal The signal to emit @data The data to attach with the signal

docs.title: ewwii_listen
docs.text:
void ewwii_listen(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * signal, CListenCallback callback)

Listen to emissions.

Listen to emissions made by other plugins and ewwii itself.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • signal The signal to listen to

  • callback The function to call when emission is found

docs.title: ewwii_register_signal
docs.text:
void ewwii_register_signal(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * name, const char * initial)

Register a signal (GlobalVar)

Register a signal (GlobalVar) to ewwii which can be accessed from configuration.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • name The name of the signal

  • initial The initial value of the signal

docs.title: ewwii_update_signal
docs.text:
void ewwii_update_signal(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * name, const char * value)

Update the value of a signal (GlobalVar)

Update the value of a signal (Global).

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • name The name of the signal to update

  • value The value to set

docs.title: ewwii_signal_value
docs.text:
void ewwii_signal_value(const struct HostHandle * handle, const char * name, void(*)(const struct HostHandle *, const char *) future_handler)

Get the value of a signal (GlobalVar)

Get the value of a signal (GlobalVar) and do callback.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• handle The host handle

  • name The name of the signal to get value of

  • future_handler The function to call back to after resolving value

docs.title: Creating your plugin
docs.text:
Since you've finished installing the C bindings, let's setup your plugin now.

You can start off by running this command:

plugc-ewwii init --name "awesome-plugin"

It will do all the hard work of setting up your plugin, Makefile, bindings, etc. After its done, you'll have a file strucutre like this:

.
├── bindings
│   ├── ewwii.h
│   └── libpluginc_ewwii.a
├── Makefile
└── src
    └── plugin.c

There exists an example plugin at src/plugin.c .You can run make you compile this plugin.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Plug In C is a comprehensive C binding for writing plugins for ewwii in the C and C++ programming languages. Most modern languages support C through FFI (Foreign Function Interface), so this project can be extended to make bindings for other languages like Go.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
There are two critical files that are required to make the C bindings work:

  1. The ewwii.h file (C API).
  2. The libpluginc-ewwii.a file (Rust bridge).

You can set it up manually, but we highly advice you to use our tool which will handle initialization, and managing of the C bindings a breeze.

You can run this command to install plugc-ewwii locally:

sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Byson94/plug-in-c.ewwii/refs/heads/main/plugc-ewwii/install.sh)"
